commit.message:
Decouple printContainerStatus via HealthChecker interface (#28)

* Add ServerManager + HealthChecker interfaces, container package, and decouple printContainerStatus

Introduces management.ServerManager interface to decouple commands from
concrete ScreenManager. Adds management.HealthChecker optional interface
so printContainerStatus type-asserts to the interface instead of
*container.Manager. Creates internal/container package with Manager
(implements both interfaces), RCON client, and tests.

commit.message:
fix: OIDC trusted  add repository URL, use Node 24, clear NODE_AUTH_TOKENpublishing

- Add repository.url to package.json (npm OIDC needs it to match GitHub)
- Bump publish workflow to Node 24 (npm >=11.5 required for OIDC)
- Clear NODE_AUTH_TOKEN on publish steps (setup-node sets it, which
  overrides OIDC token exchange)

Ref: https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/176761

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use persistent RCON connection instead of connect-per-command (#26)

* Initial plan

* Add persistent RCON connection to container.Manager

- Add ServerManager interface to management package
- Create container package with RCONClient (Source RCON protocol)
- container.Manager uses persistent RCON connection:
  - Lazy-connect on first SendCommand
  - Reuse connection for subsequent calls
  - Auto-reconnect on broken pipe, EOF, or closed connection
  - Close() method tears down the connection
  - Thread-safe via mutex
- Comprehensive tests for RCON client and container Manager

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract maxRCONBodySize constant from magic number

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: auth deadline, retry error, concurrency docs, test safety

- rcon.go: Add 5s auth handshake deadline in Connect to prevent blocking
- rcon.go: Clarify RCON protocol type constant ambiguity with comment
- rcon.go: Fix concurrency doc (Connect/Close externally synchronized)
- rcon.go: Return empty string on response ID mismatch (Go convention)
- rcon.go: Improve comment on two-packet auth handling
- container.go: Fix retry logic to return retry error, not stale original
- container.go: Add compile-time ServerManager interface check
- container_test.go: Remove direct internal field access in tests
- container_test.go: Use real server shutdown for reconnect test
- rcon_test.go: Track and close active connections in fakeRCON.close()

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace ESLint with Biome 2.4.6; migrate to pnpm; upgrade to Expo SDK 55; add Jest + Playwright testing (#1)

* Initial plan

* Scaffold Grailguard – Expo SDK 55 / React Three Fiber auto-battler

Complete project scaffold including:
- Config: package.json, tsconfig.json, app.json, babel/metro/tailwind configs
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions workflows for lint+typecheck and GitHub Pages deploy
- Engine: procedural map generator, combat logic, noise texture utils, game constants
- Stores: Zustand game store (units/buildings/gold/health) + persisted meta store
- Components: BezelLayout HUD, FloatingText, MarketModal, day/night Environment,
  instanced MapGrid, Sanctuary, pooled ParticleSystem, CombatController (useFrame
  game loop), BuildingController, UnitMesh, BuildingMesh
- Screens: main menu (index) and full game screen with drag-drop building placement,
  wave spawner, and divine smite ability
- Expo Router layout with gesture handler root

commit.message:
feat: add Google Analytics (GA4) tracking

Added gtag.js snippet to index.html with placeholder ID G-XXXXXXXXXX.
Updated CSP headers to allow googletagmanager.com and google-analytics.com.

Replace G-XXXXXXXXXX with your actual GA4 Measurement ID.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add complete Django backend with Docker infrastructure

Backend modules:
- accounts: OTP auth, JWT tokens, user profiles, emergency contacts
- hazards: PostGIS hazard pins with spatial queries, upvoting
- emergency: SOS trigger, live location tracking, event lifecycle
- privacy: OSINT scanning, DPDP Act notice generator, deepfake placeholder
- routing: Safe path scoring engine with pluggable map providers
- dashboard: Police/admin endpoints for emergency mgmt and analytics

Infrastructure:
- Docker Compose: PostgreSQL+PostGIS, Redis, Celery worker/beat, Gunicorn
- Celery tasks: emergency dispatch, OSINT scraper, deepfake analyzer
- Service layer: SMS (console/twilio/msg91), map provider abstraction
- JWT auth, CORS, rate limiting, structured logging
- DRF Spectacular API documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: HACRL local training demo notebook for Strix Halo

Implements Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning
(arXiv 2603.02604) as a runnable local training sample.

Key features:
- Two heterogeneous agents (Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B + 7B) trained simultaneously
- GRPO-style rollout generation with verifiable math rewards
- Cross-agent rollout sharing with dual-clip importance weighting
- Distribution shift monitoring (KL threshold guard)
- Asymmetric lambda scaling (weaker agent gets more from stronger)
- LoRA adapters - only ~35 GB total memory needed on Strix Halo

Strix Halo (128 GB unified memory, ROCm) can comfortably run
model pairs up to 7B + 32B.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor ServerManager.Start to Launch — remove unused command/args params (#24)

* Initial plan

* Refactor ServerManager.Start to Launch — remove unused command/args params

Implements Option 2 from the issue:
- ServerManager interface uses Launch(ctx) with no command/args
- ScreenManager stores scriptPath and uses it in Launch
- Container Manager.Launch just calls podman start (no unused params)
- All callers updated to use ServerManager interface
- Added --mode flag (auto/screen/container) for CLI
- Added container package with Manager, RCON client, and tests
- Compile-time interface compliance checks for both backends

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix lint: gofumpt formatting and hugeParam for vote.Config

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: persistent RCON, Stop fallback, validation, detectMode fix

- Fix RCONClient.Close() race: acquire mutex, nil conn after close
- Apply ctx deadline in Connect auth handshake to prevent hanging
- Make RCON persistent in container.Manager with lazy connect + reconnect

## Summary

Adds specialized agents for the repository:

- **Feature Developer agent**: Guidelines for developing new features,
extending collectors, and integrating tools
- **Docs Writer agent**: Agent for writing/updating documentation
(MkDocs, API reference, agent specs, etc.)
- **Commit Validator agent**: Pre-commit rule enforcement for code
quality, architecture contracts, test coverage, CI readiness, security,
and documentation alignment
- **Factory Commit Validator agent**: Specialized commit validator for
NetBox deployment factory changes
- **Documentation agents**: Combined documentation agents for factory
development

between VPS servers to mask client IP with host IP, while preserving

commit.message:
fix: remove secrets context from step if condition in build workflow (#335)

## Problem

Every push to main since PR #313 (Homebrew tap auto-update) has failed
with **"This run likely failed because of a workflow file issue"** —
zero jobs created.

**Root cause:** The step-level `if` condition on the "Update Homebrew
tap" step used `secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN != ''`, which causes GitHub
Actions to reject the entire workflow at parse time.

## Fix

1. **Remove `secrets` from the `if` condition** — use a runtime shell
guard (`if [ -z "$GH_TOKEN" ]`) instead
2. **Replace indented heredoc with piped echo** — the original `EOF`
delimiter was indented (wouldn't terminate properly in a YAML block
scalar)

## Verification

- Last green build:
[#22873258217](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/22873258217)
(before PR #313)
- First red build:
[#22876701610](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/22876701610)
(PR #313 merge)
- All 16+ subsequent pushes to main have failed with the same workflow
parse error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Addressing PR comments (#23)

* Initial plan

* Add internal/container package with unit tests for RCONClient and Manager

- Add container.go and rcon.go from feat/container branch
- Add comprehensive rcon_test.go: auth success/failure, connect errors,
  command execution, concurrent commands, server disconnect, empty body
- Add comprehensive container_test.go: IsRunning, Start, Stop, Session,
  Health, Stats, Exists, SendCommand using MockRunner
- Export MockRunner.Key() method for cross-package test usage

* Address review feedback: ctx deadline in auth, ID validation, flaky port fixes, runningErr test

- Apply ctx deadline to connection during auth exchange so Connect respects
  timeouts/cancellation beyond the dial phase
- Validate auth response ID matches request ID; reject mismatched or
  unexpected packet types during auth
- Replace hard-coded 127.0.0.1:1 with ephemeral port allocation
  (listen+close pattern) to eliminate environment-dependent flakiness
- Add "empty status running error" test case to exercise the runningErr
  branch in Health tests

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
openclaw: add auto-approver sidecar, remove broken dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth flag

The dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth flag caused the browser JS to skip
generating device identity, resulting in persistent 'device identity
required' errors from the gateway.

The auto-approver sidecar watches for pending device pairing requests
every 3 seconds and approves them automatically, so new browsers/devices
can pair without manual intervention.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate rate-limit thundering herd during historical candle seeding (#78)

* Initial plan

* fix: prevent thundering herd on rate limit and reduce seeding concurrency

- bot/exchange.py: After sleeping for rate limit, re-check if another
  thread already reset the window before resetting _weight_used. This
  prevents all waking threads from stampeding the API simultaneously.
- bot/bot.py: Reduce _seed_historical_candles BATCH_SIZE from 10 to 5
  and increase inter-batch sleep from 1s to 3s, keeping weight usage
  well under the 1200/min Binance Futures limit.

Co-authored-by: kishore446 <78694720+kishore446@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: audit v2 cycle 1 — REPO_ROOT fallback, hook ownership, shlex quoting

AC1-01: Add REPO_ROOT git-based fallback for Python hooks in Rust wrapper
AC1-02: Use shlex.quote() instead of bash double quotes for binary paths
AC1-03: Narrow hook_system ownership check from 'hook_system in cmd' to
        'tools/{hook_system}/' to prevent false matches on parent dirs

commit.message:
fix: scan full document for process name in runProcessCommand

Previously required cursor to be on the name line. Now parses the
entire document with a multiline regex, so the command works
regardless of cursor position.

Also remove resolved dist/ gitignore bug from CLAUDE.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: add SRI comment for CDN scripts, note pending hashes

CDN script integrity hashes should be added before production
deployment. The environment lacks outbound CDN access to compute
them automatically; hashes must be verified and added manually.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(health): add HTTP endpoint checker and service-discovery health aggregation

Enhance the health package with two new checker types:

- HTTPChecker: probes a remote HTTP endpoint with configurable timeout,
  expected status code, and custom HTTP client
- AggregateChecker: verifies a downstream service's /health endpoint returns
  status "ok" in the JSON response body
- ServiceDiscoveryChecker: queries all instances of a service from the
  discovery registry, probes each instance's health endpoint, and
  automatically marks instances healthy/unhealthy in the registry

All checkers integrate with the existing Handler and Checker interface.
Includes comprehensive test suite (16 new tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(discovery): add service discovery with round-robin load balancing

Implement in-memory service registry for microservice communication with:

- Instance registration, deregistration, and update
- Round-robin load balancing across healthy instances
- Health-status-aware routing (healthy, unhealthy, draining)
- Heartbeat tracking and stale-instance detection via MarkStale
- Static configuration support via RegisterStatic
- Instance metadata for version/region tagging
- State change callbacks for monitoring
- Thread-safe with sync.RWMutex
- Comprehensive test suite (28 tests including examples)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
